findingDetails.title: `--path` boundary docs omit the live `/tmp` test-fixture exception
findingDetails.evidence: The workflow and quick references state traversal outside the repository is rejected, while create.sh permits resolved targets under /tmp or TMPDIR for test fixtures before enforcing the repository boundary.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reword workflow references to state traversal is rejected and normal targets must stay inside the repository, with absolute /tmp/TMPDIR paths reserved for test fixtures.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Same boundary wording appears in execution_methods.md and quick_reference.md; create.sh has one resolve_and_validate_create_target gate with a /tmp/TMPDIR exception before repo-boundary rejection.

traceabilityChecks.notes: Manifest-backed Level contract, inline renderer, validation exit-code, phase-mode, and post-create validation claims checked against implementation surfaces.
Tasks and implementation-summary verification records checked against scope evidence.
SKILL.md exit-code and scaffold guidance checked.
Runtime mirror consistency reserved for later configured dimension.

noveltyJustification: First iteration produced one new P2 implementation-spec-alignment finding; weighted new findings ratio is 1/1.
edgeCases: resource-map.md absent; used implementation-summary.md and commit e60b095416 ledger
parent graph-metadata last_active_child_id has advanced to 006 after 005 and was not treated as a 005 defect
/tmp exception downgraded to P2 because implementation remains explicit and safe
ruledOut: No P0/P1 manifest-backed Level contract contradiction confirmed
No parent last_active_child_id finding opened because 005 remains in children_ids and 006 reflects later parent progress
